prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for acrolein (CH2=CH-CHO) were computed using the fukui-reactivity chemkit skill at the DFT level with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard') in PySCF. The calculation used the user-supplied geometry at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz without re-optimization (single-point only). System: gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. N±1 states: cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges by Mulliken scheme (PySCF).

All three SCF calculations converged. Charge conservation checks passed: Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000. Integrity status: trustworthy = true.

Key results (per-atom Fukui indices):
- Most electrophilic site: C4 (β-carbon), f⁺ = 0.64232102 — the dominant site for nucleophilic (Michael) addition
- Most nucleophilic site: O1 (carbonyl oxygen), f⁻ = 0.39388616 — dominant site for electrophilic activation
- Dual descriptor for C4: +0.5549512 (strongly electrophilic character confirmed)

Full per-atom results:
Atom | f⁺        | f⁻        | f⁰        | Dual
O1   | 0.40136   | 0.39389   | 0.39763   | +0.00748
C2   | 0.02226   | 0.00599   | 0.01412   | +0.01627
C3   | 0.43405   | 0.09140   | 0.26272   | +0.34264
C4   | 0.64232   | 0.08737   | 0.36485   | +0.55495
H5   | −0.15726  | 0.08596   | −0.03565  | −0.24322
H6   | −0.20720  | 0.18568   | −0.01076  | −0.39288
H7   | −0.06212  | 0.08449   | +0.01118  | −0.14660
H8   | −0.07341  | 0.06523   | −0.00409  | −0.13863

No warnings reported by the engine (all integrity checks passed informational). The headline value reported is the largest f⁺ (C4 β-carbon), 0.64232102.
